content.problemStatement:
Traversing a binary tree is involves visiting all the nodes in the tree in a specific order. The purpose of tree traversal is to access each node exactly once so that the data within the nodes can be processed. There are several methods to traverse a binary tree.

Let's learn about Pre Order traversal.

Pre-order traversal involves visiting the root node first, then recursively visiting the left subtree, and finally the right subtree.

Pseudo code:

`void preOrder(node root) {
    if (root == null) {
        return
    }
    print(root->value)
    preOrder(root->left)
    preOrder(root->right)
}
`Video Explanation
For example, check the preorder traversal of the following binary tree


TaskGiven a binary tree, complete the function preOrderTraversal to print the Preorder traversal of the tree.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer NNN — the number of nodes in the binary tree.
Next N−1N - 1N−1 lines contain three space separated characters pi,ci,Rip_i, c_i, R_ipi​,ci​,Ri​, describing the tree with edge informations - pip_ipi​ is an integer denoting the parent node of the iiith edge, cic_ici​ is child node, and the letter RiR_iRi​ denotes whether the child is left child or right child, it's value is `L` if cic_ici​ is left child of pip_ipi​, else `R`.


Output Format
Output on the single line, NNN space separated integers - the preorder traversal of the given tree.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains an integer N, the number of nodes in the binary tree. The next N‑1 lines each contain three space‑separated values: pi ci Ri, where pi is the parent node, ci is the child node, and Ri is either 'L' (ci is the left child of pi) or 'R' (ci is the right child of pi). All node values are distinct integers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing N space‑separated integers – the preorder traversal of the given tree.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Root is 1. Preorder visits 1, then left subtree (2), then right subtree (3) whose left child is 4 and right child is 5.
A single node tree; preorder is just that node.
Root 10, left child 20 (which has left child 40), then right child 30.

solution.approach: The tree is given as parent‑child relations. We store left and right child for every possible node value in two arrays. To find the root we look for the node that never appears as a child. Preorder (root‑left‑right) can be performed iteratively using a stack: push the root, then repeatedly pop a node, output it, push its right child (if any) followed by its left child (if any). This order ensures that the left subtree is processed before the right one. The algorithm runs in O(N) time per test case and uses O(N) additional memory for the child arrays and the stack.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) for all test cases combined
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) per test case for the left, right, isChild arrays and the explicit stack
